# utf8

```go
import "unicode/utf8"
```

Package utf8 implements functions and constants to support text encoded in UTF\-8. It includes functions to translate between runes and UTF\-8 byte sequences. See https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/UTF-8

## Index

## Constants

<a name="RuneError"></a>Numbers fundamental to the encoding.

```go
const (
    RuneError = '\uFFFD'     // the "error" Rune or "Unicode replacement character"
    RuneSelf  = 0x80         // characters below RuneSelf are represented as themselves in a single byte.
    MaxRune   = '\U0010FFFF' // Maximum valid Unicode code point.
    UTFMax    = 4            // maximum number of bytes of a UTF-8 encoded Unicode character.
)
```

<a name="AppendRune"></a>

## func [AppendRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L410>)

```go
func AppendRune(p []byte, r rune) []byte
```

AppendRune appends the UTF\-8 encoding of r to the end of p and returns the extended buffer. If the rune is out of range, it appends the encoding of [RuneError](<#RuneError>).

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	buf1 := utf8.AppendRune(nil, 0x10000)
	buf2 := utf8.AppendRune([]byte("init"), 0x10000)
	fmt.Println(string(buf1))
	fmt.Println(string(buf2))
}
```

#### Output

```go
𐀀
init𐀀
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="DecodeLastRune"></a>

## func [DecodeLastRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L279>)

```go
func DecodeLastRune(p []byte) (r rune, size int)
```

DecodeLastRune unpacks the last UTF\-8 encoding in p and returns the rune and its width in bytes. If p is empty it returns \([RuneError](<#RuneError>), 0\). Otherwise, if the encoding is invalid, it returns \(RuneError, 1\). Both are impossible results for correct, non\-empty UTF\-8.

An encoding is invalid if it is incorrect UTF\-8, encodes a rune that is out of range, or is not the shortest possible UTF\-8 encoding for the value. No other validation is performed.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	b := []byte("Hello, 世界")

	for len(b) > 0 {
		r, size := utf8.DecodeLastRune(b)
		fmt.Printf("%c %v\n", r, size)

		b = b[:len(b)-size]
	}
}
```

#### Output

```go
界 3
世 3
  1
, 1
o 1
l 1
l 1
e 1
H 1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="DecodeLastRuneInString"></a>

## func [DecodeLastRuneInString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L316>)

```go
func DecodeLastRuneInString(s string) (r rune, size int)
```

DecodeLastRuneInString is like [DecodeLastRune](<#DecodeLastRune>) but its input is a string. If s is empty it returns \([RuneError](<#RuneError>), 0\). Otherwise, if the encoding is invalid, it returns \(RuneError, 1\). Both are impossible results for correct, non\-empty UTF\-8.

An encoding is invalid if it is incorrect UTF\-8, encodes a rune that is out of range, or is not the shortest possible UTF\-8 encoding for the value. No other validation is performed.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	str := "Hello, 世界"

	for len(str) > 0 {
		r, size := utf8.DecodeLastRuneInString(str)
		fmt.Printf("%c %v\n", r, size)

		str = str[:len(str)-size]
	}
}
```

#### Output

```go
界 3
世 3
  1
, 1
o 1
l 1
l 1
e 1
H 1
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="DecodeRune"></a>

## func [DecodeRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L157>)

```go
func DecodeRune(p []byte) (r rune, size int)
```

DecodeRune unpacks the first UTF\-8 encoding in p and returns the rune and its width in bytes. If p is empty it returns \([RuneError](<#RuneError>), 0\). Otherwise, if the encoding is invalid, it returns \(RuneError, 1\). Both are impossible results for correct, non\-empty UTF\-8.

An encoding is invalid if it is incorrect UTF\-8, encodes a rune that is out of range, or is not the shortest possible UTF\-8 encoding for the value. No other validation is performed.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	b := []byte("Hello, 世界")

	for len(b) > 0 {
		r, size := utf8.DecodeRune(b)
		fmt.Printf("%c %v\n", r, size)

		b = b[size:]
	}
}
```

#### Output

```go
H 1
e 1
l 1
l 1
o 1
, 1
  1
世 3
界 3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="DecodeRuneInString"></a>

## func [DecodeRuneInString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L219>)

```go
func DecodeRuneInString(s string) (r rune, size int)
```

DecodeRuneInString is like [DecodeRune](<#DecodeRune>) but its input is a string. If s is empty it returns \([RuneError](<#RuneError>), 0\). Otherwise, if the encoding is invalid, it returns \(RuneError, 1\). Both are impossible results for correct, non\-empty UTF\-8.

An encoding is invalid if it is incorrect UTF\-8, encodes a rune that is out of range, or is not the shortest possible UTF\-8 encoding for the value. No other validation is performed.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	str := "Hello, 世界"

	for len(str) > 0 {
		r, size := utf8.DecodeRuneInString(str)
		fmt.Printf("%c %v\n", r, size)

		str = str[size:]
	}
}
```

#### Output

```go
H 1
e 1
l 1
l 1
o 1
, 1
  1
世 3
界 3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="EncodeRune"></a>

## func [EncodeRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L368>)

```go
func EncodeRune(p []byte, r rune) int
```

EncodeRune writes into p \(which must be large enough\) the UTF\-8 encoding of the rune. If the rune is out of range, it writes the encoding of [RuneError](<#RuneError>). It returns the number of bytes written.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	r := '世'
	buf := make([]byte, 3)

	n := utf8.EncodeRune(buf, r)

	fmt.Println(buf)
	fmt.Println(n)
}
```

#### Output

```go
[228 184 150]
3
```

</p>
</details>

<details><summary>Example (Out Of Range)</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	runes := []rune{
		// Less than 0, out of range.
		-1,
		// Greater than 0x10FFFF, out of range.
		0x110000,
		// The Unicode replacement character.
		utf8.RuneError,
	}
	for i, c := range runes {
		buf := make([]byte, 3)
		size := utf8.EncodeRune(buf, c)
		fmt.Printf("%d: %d %[2]s %d\n", i, buf, size)
	}
}
```

#### Output

```go
0: [239 191 189] � 3
1: [239 191 189] � 3
2: [239 191 189] � 3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FullRune"></a>

## func [FullRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L110>)

```go
func FullRune(p []byte) bool
```

FullRune reports whether the bytes in p begin with a full UTF\-8 encoding of a rune. An invalid encoding is considered a full Rune since it will convert as a width\-1 error rune.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	buf := []byte{228, 184, 150} // 世
	fmt.Println(utf8.FullRune(buf))
	fmt.Println(utf8.FullRune(buf[:2]))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="FullRuneInString"></a>

## func [FullRuneInString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L130>)

```go
func FullRuneInString(s string) bool
```

FullRuneInString is like FullRune but its input is a string.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	str := "世"
	fmt.Println(utf8.FullRuneInString(str))
	fmt.Println(utf8.FullRuneInString(str[:2]))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="RuneCount"></a>

## func [RuneCount](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L434>)

```go
func RuneCount(p []byte) int
```

RuneCount returns the number of runes in p. Erroneous and short encodings are treated as single runes of width 1 byte.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	buf := []byte("Hello, 世界")
	fmt.Println("bytes =", len(buf))
	fmt.Println("runes =", utf8.RuneCount(buf))
}
```

#### Output

```go
bytes = 13
runes = 9
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="RuneCountInString"></a>

## func [RuneCountInString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L447>)

```go
func RuneCountInString(s string) (n int)
```

RuneCountInString is like [RuneCount](<#RuneCount>) but its input is a string.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	str := "Hello, 世界"
	fmt.Println("bytes =", len(str))
	fmt.Println("runes =", utf8.RuneCountInString(str))
}
```

#### Output

```go
bytes = 13
runes = 9
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="RuneLen"></a>

## func [RuneLen](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L347>)

```go
func RuneLen(r rune) int
```

RuneLen returns the number of bytes in the UTF\-8 encoding of the rune. It returns \-1 if the rune is not a valid value to encode in UTF\-8.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	fmt.Println(utf8.RuneLen('a'))
	fmt.Println(utf8.RuneLen('界'))
}
```

#### Output

```go
1
3
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="RuneStart"></a>

## func [RuneStart](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L457>)

```go
func RuneStart(b byte) bool
```

RuneStart reports whether the byte could be the first byte of an encoded, possibly invalid rune. Second and subsequent bytes always have the top two bits set to 10.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	buf := []byte("a界")
	fmt.Println(utf8.RuneStart(buf[0]))
	fmt.Println(utf8.RuneStart(buf[1]))
	fmt.Println(utf8.RuneStart(buf[2]))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="Valid"></a>

## func [Valid](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L470>)

```go
func Valid(p []byte) bool
```

Valid reports whether p consists entirely of valid UTF\-8\-encoded runes.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	valid := []byte("Hello, 世界")
	invalid := []byte{0xff, 0xfe, 0xfd}

	fmt.Println(utf8.Valid(valid))
	fmt.Println(utf8.Valid(invalid))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ValidRune"></a>

## func [ValidRune](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L570>)

```go
func ValidRune(r rune) bool
```

ValidRune reports whether r can be legally encoded as UTF\-8. Code points that are out of range or a surrogate half are illegal.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	valid := 'a'
	invalid := rune(0xfffffff)

	fmt.Println(utf8.ValidRune(valid))
	fmt.Println(utf8.ValidRune(invalid))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
```

</p>
</details>

<a name="ValidString"></a>

## func [ValidString](<https://github.com/Homebrew/brew/blob/main/opt/homebrew/Cellar/go/1.26.1/libexec/src/unicode/utf8/utf8.go#L522>)

```go
func ValidString(s string) bool
```

ValidString reports whether s consists entirely of valid UTF\-8\-encoded runes.

<details><summary>Example</summary>
<p>

```go
package main

import (
	"fmt"
	"unicode/utf8"
)

func main() {
	valid := "Hello, 世界"
	invalid := string([]byte{0xff, 0xfe, 0xfd})

	fmt.Println(utf8.ValidString(valid))
	fmt.Println(utf8.ValidString(invalid))
}
```

#### Output

```go
true
false
```

</p>
</details>
